values.value:
== PROFILE RULES == Profiles have TWO paths depending on the operation.
-- PATH 1: CLONE (use dmlOperations) -- { "type": "profileClone", "sourceProfileName": "Sales User", "newProfileName": "Custom Sales User", "description": "Optional description" }
-- PATH 2: PARTIAL UPDATE (generate XML in files[]) -- File path: profiles/ProfileApiName.profile metadataType: Profile memberName: ProfileApiName
Only include sections that match what the ticket asks for.
-- OBJECT ACCESS -- <objectPermissions> <allowCreate>true</allowCreate> <allowDelete>false</allowDelete> <allowEdit>true</allowEdit> <allowRead>true</allowRead> <modifyAllRecords>false</modifyAllRecords> <object>MyObject__c</object> <viewAllRecords>false</viewAllRecords> </objectPermissions>
-- FIELD ACCESS -- <fieldPermissions> <editable>true</editable> <field>MyObject__c.MyField__c</field> <readable>true</readable> </fieldPermissions>
-- APEX CLASS ACCESS -- Query org state to find class names. <classAccesses> <apexClass>MyClassName</apexClass> <enabled>true</enabled> </classAccesses> RULES: - apexClass value is the class API name - enabled=true to grant, enabled=false to revoke - Multiple classes = multiple <classAccesses> blocks in ONE profile file
-- TAB VISIBILITY -- Use ONLY when ticket explicitly asks to show/hide a tab for the profile. DO NOT add tabVisibilities just because you are granting object access. <tabVisibilities> <tab>ObjectName__c</tab> <visibility>DefaultOn</visibility> </tabVisibilities> visibility values: DefaultOn, DefaultOff, Hidden
-- FULL EXAMPLE -- <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Profile xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <classAccesses> <apexClass>ReservationController</apexClass> <enabled>true</enabled> </classAccesses> <objectPermissions> <allowCreate>true</allowCreate> <allowDelete>false</allowDelete> <allowEdit>true</allowEdit> <allowRead>true</allowRead> <modifyAllRecords>false</modifyAllRecords> <object>Reservation__c</object> <viewAllRecords>false</viewAllRecords> </objectPermissions> </Profile>
CRITICAL PROFILE RULES: 1. NEVER add <tabVisibilities> unless ticket explicitly asks to show/hide a tab 2. NEVER reference a CustomTab that does not exist — it will cause a deploy failure 3. Only include profile sections that match what the ticket asks for 4. For DELETE: use destructiveFiles with metadataType Profile and member = profile API name 5. Standard/system profiles (Admin, Standard, StandardAul, MarketingProfile etc.) CANNOT be deleted
TABVISIBILITIES ABSOLUTE RULES: - NEVER add <tabVisibilities> for ANY standard object (Account, Case, Contact, Lead, Opportunity etc.) - Standard objects already have built-in tabs — referencing them as CustomTab fails with "no CustomTab named X found" - ONLY add <tabVisibilities> for custom objects (__c suffix) AND only when ticket explicitly asks - When in doubt → OMIT <tabVisibilities> entirely
Profile XML: object access, field access, class access, tab visibility
